rulings.Case Title: PAVEL NIDERMAN VS. WILLIAM CHILD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant'S Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 3, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT WILLIAM CHILD'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Defendant's Discovery Requests; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel discovery granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff to serve responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, within 15 days of notice of the court's order granting the motion to compel. Good cause appearing, sanctions in the amount of $857.58 awarded in favor of defendant William Child, against plaintiff Pavel Niderman; plaintiff to pay sanctions to defendant within 15 days of notice of the court's order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KEN LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to maintain its initial summary judgment/summary adjudication burden. Plaintiff may be able to establish the breach of contract and bad faith claims. The evidence indicates that Plaintiff's walnut tree may have been covered, but defendant never investigated that portion of the damage. Defendant's PMK regarding claims handling admitted that the tree was not properly investigated. (Guerra Depo., 131:25-135:13). The court denies summary adjudication as to the remaining claims. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Wang was acting as Defendant's agent. "The most definitive characteristic of an insurance agent is his authority to bind his principal, the insurer; an insurance broker has no such authority." Marsh & McLennan of Cal., Inc. v City of LA (1976) 62 Cal.App.3d 108, 117. Here, Mr. Wang testified that he used his binding authority to procure Plaintiff's policy from Defendant. (Paul Wang Depo., 280:3-9; 282:11-19; 283:7-21). Even Defendant admits that Wang had such binding authority. (Smith Dec., par. 15). If there is an agency relationship, then Defendant is vicariously liable for Wang's misconduct/negligence. It is possible that Wang was acting as a dual agent and Kurtz, Richards, Wilson & Co. v. Ins. Communicators Marketing Corp. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1249, 1257, notes that such relationships are not uncommon. The bad faith claim based on a reformed policy is viable because coverage was available under the original or reformed version of the policy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: WINCO WINDOW COMPANY INC., A CORPORATION VS. PROGRESS GLASS CO. INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2015, Line 8, CROSS COMPLAINANT PROGRESS GLASS COMPANY INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint. Defendant/Cross-complainant Progress Glass Company's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. If Plaintiff/Cross-defendant Winco Window Company seeks to continue the trial in this matter, in may do so via an ex parte motion in Department 206. " '[A]llegations of fraud involve a serious attack on character, and fairness to the defendant demands that he should receive the fullest possible details of the charge in order to prepare his defense.' " (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216.) Progress Glass' amended complaint alleges that Winco represented from March to June 2013 that it would ship glazing to Progress in June 2013. This representation appears to have been knowingly false. (Deposition of Rudy Selinger, p. 110.) Progress Glass relied on those misrepresentations and then incurred $500,000 in damages. Progress Glass' amended pleading, therefore, states a valid cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D SUMNER et al VS. ARA AROUSTAMIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF JAMES SUMNER, ANTHONY MOROYAN'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Notice of Motion and Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Amending plaintiff's motion to include a claim for malicious prosecution based on a lawsuit filed in Armenia will cause significant time delay, trial expenses, and prejudice to defendants. Defendant Aroustamian was not even a party to the Armenian action. Furthermore, whether a lawsuit filed in Armenia lacked probable cause requires knowledge of Armenian law that is not required for the California State Bar. In the instant case, it is improper to burden this Court with a complaint that necessitates sufficient knowledge of Armenian law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MYXOLIDIAN, LLC VS. WALTER J. CLARKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT WALTER CLARKE, JOHN ROSENFELD, OXFORD INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Defendants' Application of Daniel G. Dowd for Admission to Appear as Counsel Pro Hac Vice is GRANTED. Defendants have not stated whether Dowd has provided informed consent to his investor clients. At this stage Plaintiff's opposition is premature as Dowd meets the criteria for pro hac vice admission under California Rules of Court, Rule 9.40(f). Plaintiffs may later choose contest Defendants' counsel through a properly noticed Motion to Disqualify. Should an unwaivable conflict of interest arise in the future, this Court has the inherent power to revoke Dowd's pro hac vice status should he engage in conduct that would be sufficient to disqualify a California attorney. (Sheller v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1697, 1716.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
